message:
perf(showcase/quake): precompute mesh + once-per-frame world→camera transform

Hoists per-triangle work out of the render hot loop in the e1m1 quake
showcase screen. Fewer cosines, fewer dots, fewer normalizations,
fewer palette lookups per frame — same exact image.

The render path used to do, for every visible triangle:
- load three indices, fetch three packed vertices
- multiply each by `1.0 / 1024.0` and Vec3-construct (3×)
- compute `(w1-w0).cross(w2-w0).normalized()` to derive a face normal
- dot the normal against the same light_dir on every triangle
- recompute `(w0.z - bounds_min.z) / height_span` and call
  `palette_quake_stone(...)` to derive the base color
- subtract `eye` from each world vertex and dot it against
  right/up/forward (3 vertices × 3 dot products = 9 dots)

All of that is either:
1. A property of the mesh (vertices, normals, base color, the diffuse
   factor `n·light_dir`) — fixed for the lifetime of the level, and
2. A property of the camera frame (world→camera basis transform) —
   the same for every triangle inside one render call.

Now both sides are amortized:

QuakeE1M1State adds three new fields:
- `world_vertices: Vec<Vec3>` — the QUAKE_E1M1_VERTS table converted
  once at construction (in `Self::build_render_mesh`) into Vec3 with
  the `1.0 / 1024.0` scale already applied.
- `camera_vertices: Vec<Vec3>` — a buffer the same length as
  world_vertices, written by the per-frame world→camera pass below.
  Initialized to zeros in `Default::default`; resized only if the
  mesh ever grows (currently it doesn't).
- `render_tris: Vec<RenderTri>` — one per triangle in QUAKE_E1M1_TRIS,
  carrying the three vertex indices, the precomputed normal, the
  precomputed `n·light_dir` (`diffuse`), and the precomputed
  `palette_quake_stone(height_t)` base color packed into PackedRgba.

Per-frame work in the render closure now collapses to:

  1. One single pass over `world_vertices` to fill `camera_vertices`
     using the new basis: `cam = (w - eye) · {right, up, forward}`.
     This replaces nine dot products *per visible triangle* with three
     dot products *per vertex* (vertices are shared between triangles,
     so this is a strict reduction).
  2. Per-triangle: just three index lookups into the camera buffer
     plus the precomputed `tri.normal`, `tri.diffuse`, `tri.base`.
     The view-dir / facing / rim computations stay the same shape.

Behavioral parity:

- `light_dir` is now used only at mesh build time (`(0.4, -0.6,
  0.5).normalized()` is still the lighting direction; it just lives
  inside `build_render_mesh` instead of the hot loop).
- `inv_scale` similarly moves from the hot loop to mesh init.
- The height-based `palette_quake_stone(height_t)` lookup is now
  baked into RenderTri::base. Same input, same output, computed once.
- `step_by(tri_step)` is preserved — the FxQuality switch still picks
  step=0 (off) vs step=1, just over `self.render_tris` instead of
  the static QUAKE_E1M1_TRIS slice. Unchanged frame composition.

The `camera_vertices.len() != world_vertices.len()` guard keeps the
buffer correctly sized if a future change ever rebuilds the mesh
mid-game; today it's a no-op.

No public API changes; no new deps; no .insta snapshot updates
required because the shaded output for a given viewpoint is
identical bytewise (we're just computing the same numbers in a
different order).
